topic: Antarctic gravity hole — post-c8 patch verification (duplicated sentence, wrong-paragraph fix, partial arithmetic propagation)
change_summary: c8 patches applied by decider-self-apply (commit a24e6ab): (a) detail_evidence paragraph-1 'Either way, the model's own height function is off by two to three orders of magnitude' → 'Either way, the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by 170–1,430× depending on whether the comparison is apples-to-apples (absolute m/s²) or dimensionless-ratio (fractional variation)'; (b) detail_evidence paragraph-1 '~0.006 m/s²' → '0.0062 m/s²' (one occurrence); (c) c8 H4 (Domain-4 r=20,015 falsification-violation promotion) + H5 (Mascon-SAA correlation rebuttal) routed to analyst via EXP-221 (acknowledged, not flagged here). What the patch did NOT do: (i) replacement introduced a duplicated sentence — 'This is not a matter of calibration or measurement uncertainty; it is a qualitative failure of the dome's gravity mechanism.' now appears twice consecutively in detail_evidence (confirmed in both data/wins.json AND docs/index.html on main); (ii) the WGS84-tautology paragraph's closing sentence still reads 'the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by 170×' — this is the literal sentence c8 H1 called out as the reader-trap, and it was not touched; the 170–1,430× range was inserted in paragraph 1 instead; (iii) the '0.006 → 0.0062' propagation covered only detail_evidence; detail_verdict_text, detail_extra paragraph (ii), and tldr_evidence still carry '~0.006 m/s²' and '0.063%' (which requires 0.0062 to round-trip).
our_argument_summary: Unchanged structurally from c8. The WIN's argument — dome's H(r) chain predicts 90% gravity loss at Antarctic rim under linear coupling (38% under H^(1/5)), observed variation is 0.53%, apples-to-apples miss is 1,430× at the ~70°S conservative anchor (5,600× at true interior), and the only escape collapses Domain 4 into a WGS84 tautology — remains sound. The c8 patches targeted framing/precision carry-forwards rather than argument structure, so the verdict Self-Contradicted continues to hold. Confidence drops from 0.88 (c8) to 0.82 because the patch introduced a NEW visible defect (duplicated sentence published on the live site) and because c8 H1 was marked ISS-1382 'fixed' without actually fixing the sentence c8 H1 targeted. The load-bearing arguments are intact; the execution of the patch cycle has a process failure worth calling out.

relevant_files: docs/index.html (rendered WIN-067 panel — confirmed duplicated sentence on live site)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Unchanged from c8. Domain 4 g(r)=9.7803·(1+0.005307·exp(−r/λg)) with λg=8,619 km; at r=0 reduces to WGS84 polar gravity 9.8322 m/s². Domain 19 one-sided threshold 'Anomaly <20 mGal' against East-Antarctic Bouguer −40 to −60 mGal is pass-by-threshold, not pass-by-prediction.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: No change from c8. monitoring='none' remains the conservative classification; Domain 19 threshold-gate is borderline but the current label is the weaker-for-us choice and fine as-is.
data_sources_used: WGS84 reference ellipsoid normal gravity formula (Somigliana)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Domain 4 falsification 'Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard' calibrates to the globe rather than testing the dome. Domain 19 one-sided threshold inverts falsification logic.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None beyond carry-forward items already noted in c6/c7/c8. No new code claims were introduced in the c8 patch cycle.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Unchanged from c8. wins.json retains monitoring='none' — no live API fetch of Antarctic gravity data into the dome pipeline; Domain 4 is WGS84-equivalent and Domain 19 is a threshold gate, not a live-data comparator.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c8. Antarctic gravity low is explained by mantle plume + incomplete PGIA + dynamic topography (Pappa 2019, Ferraccioli 2011). Dome 'Sub-terrestrial structure' is a label without equations.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c8. GRACE Level-2 products continuous since 2002; Ferraccioli 2011 and Pappa 2019 are pre-commit indexed. Legitimate-novelty carve-out does not apply.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c8. Domain 4 reuses WGS84 constants (9.7803, 0.005307); at r=0 reproduces WGS84 polar by construction. 8,619 km decay length matches H(r) but is never exercised at non-degenerate r by the automation. Domain 19 is a threshold, not a derivation.

holes_found.description: NEW DEFECT INTRODUCED BY c8 PATCH — duplicated sentence on the live site. The c8 sed/replace that swapped 'Either way, the model's own height function is off by two to three orders of magnitude.' for 'Either way, the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by 170–1,430× depending on whether the comparison is apples-to-apples (absolute m/s²) or dimensionless-ratio (fractional variation).' failed to also strip the trailing 'This is not a matter of calibration or measurement uncertainty; it is a qualitative failure of the dome's gravity mechanism.' sentence from the replacement block — the replacement text apparently re-included that sentence, and the surrounding text kept it too. Result: detail_evidence now contains 'This is not a matter of calibration or measurement uncertainty; it is a qualitative failure of the dome's gravity mechanism. This is not a matter of calibration or measurement uncertainty; it is a qualitative failure of the dome's gravity mechanism.' — verbatim duplicate, two occurrences in immediate succession. Confirmed on main at commit a24e6ab in BOTH data/wins.json (2 occurrences via grep -o) AND docs/index.html (2 occurrences via grep -o). The duplicate is published on the live site and any reader scanning the WIN detail will spot it instantly. A dome defender quoting this has a free credibility hit ('your review cannot even copy-edit itself, why should we trust your arithmetic?').
holes_found.recommendation: Delete the second (duplicate) 'This is not a matter of calibration or measurement uncertainty; it is a qualitative failure of the dome's gravity mechanism.' sentence from detail_evidence. The remaining single occurrence is correct and should stand. Fix in data/wins.json; docs/index.html will regenerate on next build. This is a 1-line Edit (identical strings separated by ' '); no argument change required. Should be self-applied by decider within minutes of this review landing.

holes_found.description: ISS-1382 CLOSED-BUT-NOT-FIXED — c8 H1 carry-forward persists. ISS-1382 description paraphrased c8 H1 as 'closing sentence says vague "two to three orders of magnitude"' and patched the 'Either way...' sentence in paragraph 1 (the apples-to-apples/dimensionless reconciliation sentence). But c8 H1 explicitly targeted a DIFFERENT sentence: the closing sentence of the WGS84-tautology paragraph — 'the formula that works is wholly WGS84; the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by 170×.' That sentence is still verbatim in detail_evidence at commit a24e6ab (confirmed via grep 'fails by 170' — two hits, one in paragraph 1 with the new '170–1,430×' range AND one in paragraph 2 still saying only 'fails by 170×.' with no range). A reader following the text linearly lands on '1,430× error' (para 1, apples-to-apples), '≈ 170×' (para 1, dimensionless), 'fails by 170–1,430×' (para 1, reconciliation — GOOD, new), standard-physics rebuttal, and then 1,500+ characters later at the end of para 2 'fails by 170×' ALONE — reverting to the 170× figure without the range qualifier. This IS the c8 H1 reader-trap, unresolved. The decider's patch was helpful (it strengthened para 1) but did not close the issue as described in c8 H1. ISS-1382 should be re-opened.
holes_found.recommendation: Patch the closing sentence of the WGS84-tautology paragraph 'the formula that works is wholly WGS84; the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by 170×.' to match paragraph 1's reconciliation. Two acceptable forms: (a) 'the formula that works is wholly WGS84; the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by 170–1,430× (dimensionless vs apples-to-apples; see above).' or (b) 'the formula that works is wholly WGS84; the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by at least 170× (1,430× apples-to-apples at Antarctic latitude).' Either form closes the reader-trap. Reopen ISS-1382 with this concrete target sentence.

holes_found.description: ISS-1383 INCOMPLETE FIX — c8 H2 arithmetic-precision propagation covered only one of four occurrences. The 0.006 → 0.0062 substitution was applied to detail_evidence paragraph 1 ('0.0062 m/s² — a 1,430× error') but NOT to: (1) detail_verdict_text: 'the observed drop is ~0.006 m/s² — a 1,430× error' (1,430× requires 0.0062 to round-trip; 8.87 / 0.006 = 1,478, not 1,430); (2) detail_extra paragraph (ii): 'is ~0.006 m/s² below the polar reference, a 0.063% reduction' (0.006 / 9.8322 = 0.061%, not 0.063%; consistency requires 0.0062); (3) tldr_evidence: 'measured gravity is just 0.006 m/s² (0.063%) below the polar reference. Dividing the dome's predicted 8.87 m/s² drop (linear coupling) by this 0.006 m/s² measured drop gives 1,430×' (same mismatch, twice). Any of these three remaining 0.006 figures can be quoted by a hostile reader against the 0.0062 in detail_evidence to show our arithmetic doesn't round-trip. Severity is minor because the argument is unchanged, but the process-miss (c8 H2 recommendation text explicitly listed 'detail_evidence and tldr_evidence' — tldr_evidence was skipped) is worth noting.
holes_found.recommendation: Run a field-by-field replace: in detail_verdict_text change '~0.006 m/s²' → '0.0062 m/s²'; in detail_extra paragraph (ii) change '~0.006 m/s²' → '0.0062 m/s²' (single occurrence); in tldr_evidence change both 'just 0.006 m/s² (0.063%)' → 'just 0.0062 m/s² (0.063%)' AND 'this 0.006 m/s² measured drop' → 'this 0.0062 m/s² measured drop'. Reopen ISS-1383 with this scope.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD c6 H5 — Shepherd 2012 citation precision. detail_evidence still cites 'incomplete post-glacial isostatic adjustment from the Last Glacial Maximum ice sheet, measured directly by GRACE satellite gravimetry (Shepherd et al., 2012, Science)' (DOI 10.1126/science.1228102). That DOI resolves to 'A Reconciled Estimate of Ice-Sheet Mass Balance' — a meta-analysis using GRACE+ICESat+altimetry that depends on PGIA corrections but is not primarily a PGIA-characterisation paper. Whitehouse et al. 2012 (Geophys. J. Int.) or King et al. 2012 (Nature) would be PGIA-primary. Unaddressed across c6/c7/c8/c9 patch cycles — not on any EXP list, not in ISS-1382/1383/1384. Severity remains minor (overall mechanism claim is scientifically correct) but this is now a four-cycle-old carry-forward.
holes_found.recommendation: Swap the DOI to Whitehouse 2012 (doi.org/10.1111/j.1365-246X.2012.05557.x) or King 2012 (doi.org/10.1038/nature11621), OR soften the attribution to 'constrained by GRACE satellite gravimetry (Shepherd et al., 2012, Science, using GRACE mass-balance products that depend on PGIA corrections)'. Consider opening a fresh low-priority issue so this doesn't drop off the radar between patch cycles.

holes_found.description: ADVOCATE-MODE (for balance, honoring the user-preferred 'consider the other side') — the c8 patch may be over-applied in one direction. The new paragraph-1 sentence 'Either way, the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by 170–1,430× depending on whether the comparison is apples-to-apples (absolute m/s²) or dimensionless-ratio (fractional variation).' implies that 170× and 1,430× are coordinate readings on a single spectrum — but they are actually computed on DIFFERENT baselines (170× uses the whole-globe pole-equator spread as the denominator; 1,430× uses the observed Antarctic drop from polar). A sharp dome defender could argue we are conflating apples (fractional on a global spread) with oranges (absolute at Antarctic latitude) under a rhetorical 'either way' umbrella. The c8 H1 recommendation text actually called this out with the 'understates the true discrepancy' qualifier preserved just before it. The patch as shipped has 'Either way... depending on whether the comparison is apples-to-apples (absolute m/s²) or dimensionless-ratio (fractional variation)' which mostly covers it, but doesn't explicitly note that the 170× is a LOWER bound and the 1,430× is the honest latitude-anchored figure. Not a fresh hole — the preceding sentence already says '170× ... understates the true discrepancy' — but the 'depending on' framing is slightly weaker than 'at-least 170× / apples-to-apples 1,430×' framing would be.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional framing strengthening (not a hole): consider 'Either way, the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by at least 170× (dimensionless-fractional lower bound) and by 1,430× under the apples-to-apples comparison at Antarctic latitude.' replaces the 'depending on' with explicit 'at least' bounding. Low priority — the current wording is defensible and the qualifying clauses around it do the work. Flagging for transparency, not as a required fix.

stronger_arguments.description: None introduced this cycle beyond what c8 already routed to EXP-221 (Domain-4 r=20,015 falsification-violation body promotion + Mascon-SAA shared-cause rebuttal). EXP-221 is queued and will flow through analyst on its own schedule; no duplication here.

kernel_of_truth.description: Unchanged from c8. The Mascon Gravity ↔ SAA Boundary r=0.92 correlation IS a real statistical regularity reflecting shared East-Antarctic mantle structure; the dome author's identification is not trivially wrong. The 8,619 km length scale is internally self-consistent across dome equations even if unjustified.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: A correlation is not a mechanism; shared mantle causation produces both signatures via standard geophysics. The self-contradiction verdict stands on the H(r)↔g fork — either H(r) drives gravity (fail by 170×–1,430×) or it doesn't (Domain 4 is a WGS84 tautology with no dome content). The c8 patches do not change this core logic; they sharpen framing.

deeper_analysis: This c9 review is a process-verification pass for c8 issues marked 'fixed' by decider-self-apply in commit a24e6ab. Two of the three closed issues (ISS-1382 targeting c8 H1, ISS-1383 targeting c8 H2) were closed but the underlying defects persist, and the patch that was applied introduced a new major defect (a duplicated sentence now published on the live site). Specifically: (1) ISS-1382 was reworded in the decider's issue description from 'closing sentence of the WGS84-tautology paragraph' (c8 H1 literal target) to 'closing sentence says vague two to three orders of magnitude' (which is the paragraph-1 'Either way' sentence, a DIFFERENT sentence). The decider patched the paragraph-1 sentence — an improvement — but the paragraph-2 'fails by 170×' reader-trap c8 H1 actually targeted is still present verbatim. (2) ISS-1383 replaced only one of four 0.006→0.0062 occurrences; c8 H2 recommendation text explicitly said 'throughout detail_evidence and tldr_evidence (quoted twice in detail_evidence and once in tldr_evidence)' plus detail_verdict_text and detail_extra for consistency. Three occurrences remain unfixed. (3) The paragraph-1 patch introduced a visible duplicated sentence — the 'Either way' replacement text apparently kept the downstream 'This is not a matter of calibration...' sentence in its replacement block while the untouched surrounding text also retained it, producing a copy-paste artifact now on the live site. The verdict 'Self-Contradicted' continues to hold on argument-structure grounds — c8 patches did not change the argument and c9 finds no new argument-structure weaknesses. Confidence at 0.82 reflects an argument-intact/execution-imperfect state: the WIN is well-defended, but the patch-apply pipeline missed the actual target sentence of c8 H1 and shipped a visible copy-paste defect. Recommend decider re-opens ISS-1382 with the paragraph-2 closing sentence as the literal target, re-opens ISS-1383 with the three remaining 0.006 occurrences enumerated, and opens a new minor issue for the duplicate-sentence removal (single sed is sufficient). The 'consider the other side' framing lens notes that the c8-patched 'Either way, depending on whether...' sentence is slightly weaker than 'at-least 170× / 1,430× apples-to-apples' framing would be — but this is a polish concern, not an argumentative hole.
